context.project_description: Implement token usage tracking for LLM queries to monitor costs by counting input and output tokens per query, leveraging existing instrumentation and logging systems.
key_requirements: Track input and output tokens separately for each chat operation
Aggregate token counts per query and log them using structured logging
Integrate with existing InstrumentedProxy system for transparent method wrapping
constraints: Must work within existing codebase patterns without creating new instrumentation modules
Token counting must be implemented at the LLM provider level where chat methods are defined
Use only existing logging infrastructure and protocols already present in the codebase
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/logging/logger.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
stakeholders: developer responsible for tracking LLM costs per query
system operator needing visibility into token usage for billing/optimization
existing users of the instrumentation framework who may be affected by changes

in_scope: token counting for chat operations (chat and chat_stream methods)
per-query level tracking of input/output tokens
integration with existing InstrumentedProxy system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
structured logging of token usage metrics via existing logger infrastructure
out_of_scope: token counting for embedding or reranking operations
per-call granularity beyond per-query tracking (for MVP)
creation of new instrumentation modules under /llm/instrumentation/
exposing token cost data through programmatic APIs beyond logging output
model-specific token counting logic implementation in separate files

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/logging/logger.py — Unified logging setup for the entire Fitz project
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls

assumptions.assumption: Token counting is primarily needed for chat operations (chat and chat_stream methods)
assumptions.impact: If tokens are also needed for embedding/reranking operations, tracking scope would need expansion; if other operations should count, the aggregation approach would change

assumptions.assumption: The user wants visibility into LLM cost at the per-query level
assumptions.impact: If users want different granularity (per-call vs per-query), the aggregation approach and state management logic would need to be modified

assumptions.assumption: Existing core instrumentation framework can support token tracking with minimal additions
assumptions.impact: If existing infrastructure cannot be leveraged, new modules or hooks would need to be created from scratch, increasing complexity and scope inflation

assumptions.assumption: Input + output tokens combined as total usage is sufficient for MVP
assumptions.impact: If users want separate tracking of input/output tokens or different aggregation logic, the implementation would need to be adjusted accordingly

assumptions.assumption: Logging system via existing logger infrastructure (fitz_ai/logging/logger.py) is appropriate for storing token usage data
assumptions.impact: If users want structured access beyond logging output or different storage mechanisms, additional APIs or systems would need to be built

assumptions.assumption: Per-query tracking granularity is sufficient for MVP
assumptions.impact: If per-call tracking is required instead, the state management logic and tracking approach would need modification

approaches.name: Extend Base Protocols with Structured Responses
approaches.description: Modify all provider protocols in base.py to return structured response objects that include token usage metadata, then update concrete implementations to parse and expose this data.
pros: Provides a clean, consistent interface for token tracking across all providers
Maintains backward compatibility through careful protocol extension
Enables comprehensive cost tracking from the ground up
cons: Requires significant refactoring of existing provider implementations
Breaking change to core interfaces that affects all consumers
Increases complexity of method signatures and return types
best_for: Large-scale systems requiring precise cost tracking and observability
Organizations with strict budget control requirements for LLM usage

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Aggregation Layer
approaches.description: Create a wrapping layer that instruments provider calls to capture token data using existing BenchmarkHook infrastructure, requiring minimal changes to core provider logic.
pros: Minimal impact on existing provider codebase
Leverages existing instrumentation framework without major architectural changes
Can be added incrementally with gradual adoption
cons: Instrumentation hooks receive raw strings from current providers, making token extraction impossible
Does not solve the fundamental issue that providers don't return usage data in first place
Cannot capture token usage without modifying provider interfaces to return structured responses
best_for: Systems with limited ability to modify core LLM provider logic

architecture.recommended: Extend Base Protocols with Structured Responses
architecture.reasoning: The instrumentation approach is fundamentally flawed because all existing providers return raw strings without token metadata, so hooks cannot extract usage data. The base protocol contracts must be extended to support structured responses that include token counts before any tracking mechanism can work. This approach provides comprehensive coverage and proper architectural grounding for future cost analytics while maintaining clean separation of concerns.

key_tradeoffs.Provider Interface Changes: Modifying base protocol interfaces to return structured responses with token metadata breaks backward compatibility but enables comprehensive tracking
key_tradeoffs.Instrumentation Limitations: Current instrumentation system can only track method calls and timing, not extract token usage from string returns without changing provider contracts
key_tradeoffs.State Management: Aggregating tokens across multiple LLM calls requires either context propagation or explicit state management which isn't currently implemented in the architecture

technology_considerations: Protocol-based design for LLM providers needs extension to support structured responses with token metadata
Instrumentation system requires modification to capture and aggregate numerical usage data across method calls
Answer model must be extended with token tracking fields for end-to-end visibility
Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, and Cohere provider implementations need updates to parse and expose API response token usage
Thread-local or request-scoped context management needed for cross-call token aggregation within single queries
architecture.scope_statement: Refactor LLM provider interfaces to return structured response objects including token metadata, update all concrete implementations to parse and expose usage data from API responses, and modify the FitzKrag engine to aggregate and report token usage per query.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend LLM Provider Protocols with Token Usage Tracking
adrs.context: The current LLM provider protocols in base.py do not support token usage tracking. All providers return simple types (str, list[float]) without metadata about token consumption. This makes it impossible to track and report on LLM costs or optimize usage patterns.
adrs.decision: Modify all core LLM provider interfaces (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider, VisionProvider) in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py to return structured response objects that include token usage information. Create a ChatResponse tuple and extend other protocols accordingly.
adrs.rationale: This approach maintains backward compatibility through careful protocol extension while enabling comprehensive token tracking across all LLM integrations. It leverages existing infrastructure patterns in the codebase (protocols, dataclasses) without requiring major architectural overhauls. The solution is consistent with how other structured responses are handled in the system.
consequences: All concrete provider implementations must be updated to return structured response objects instead of raw strings
API layer changes needed to extract and report token usage in QueryResponse
Engine answer method requires modifications to aggregate token counts across multiple LLM calls
Instrumentation hooks can now capture token usage data from enhanced provider responses
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Modify providers to return tuples (str, int) instead of structured objects - rejected because it's less extensible and doesn't support detailed breakdowns like prompt vs completion tokens
Alternative 2 - Add global state tracking via thread-local storage - rejected because this violates the principle of explicit data flow and creates hard-to-debug side effects
Alternative 3 - Extend instrumentation system to parse raw responses for token usage - rejected because current providers don't expose usage metadata in their return values

adrs.title: ADR: Implement Token Usage Aggregation in FitzKragEngine
adrs.context: The FitzKragEngine.answer method orchestrates multiple LLM calls (chat, embedding, reranking) but doesn't aggregate token usage across these operations. Users cannot see the total cost of processing a single query.
adrs.decision: Modify the FitzKragEngine.answer method to collect and aggregate token counts from each LLM call within a query execution context. Store accumulated token usage in the final Answer object for reporting purposes.
adrs.rationale: This approach provides end-to-end visibility into query costs by aggregating usage data at the engine level where all LLM interactions are coordinated. It requires minimal changes to existing logic while providing immediate value to users who need cost tracking capabilities.
consequences: Answer objects must be extended with token usage fields (e.g., token_count, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens)
Engine answer method becomes more complex as it now tracks cross-call state
API responses can include detailed token usage metrics for query processing
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Use thread-local storage or global context managers to track tokens across calls - rejected because of complexity and potential side effects that violate architectural principles
Alternative 2 - Require each LLM call site to manually report usage - rejected because it's error-prone, violates DRY principle, and creates maintenance burden
Alternative 3 - Add token tracking as optional instrumentation only - rejected because this would be incomplete without comprehensive coverage of all LLM interactions

adrs.title: ADR: Update API Layer to Report Token Usage in QueryResponse
adrs.context: The current API endpoint at fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py returns QueryResponse objects that do not include token usage information even though processing may involve multiple LLM calls with associated costs.
adrs.decision: Extend the QueryResponse data structure and update query() and chat() endpoints to populate token usage metrics from engine answers before returning responses. This provides users with immediate visibility into cost implications of their queries.
adrs.rationale: This approach delivers token tracking value at the API boundary where it's most useful for end-users. It follows existing patterns in the codebase where data structures are extended to include additional metadata without breaking backward compatibility.
consequences: API response format changes to include token_usage field
Backward compatibility maintained through optional token usage fields
Improved observability and cost awareness for API consumers
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Only log token usage internally without exposing in responses - rejected because it defeats the purpose of providing actionable cost information to users
Alternative 2 - Create separate endpoint for token reporting - rejected because this creates unnecessary complexity and poor UX by requiring two calls to understand query costs
Alternative 3 - Implement token tracking as optional feature that can be disabled - rejected because it would create inconsistent behavior based on configuration

adrs.title: ADR: Create Standardized TokenUsage Data Structure for Consistent Tracking
adrs.context: No standardized data structure exists in the codebase to represent token usage information. Providers currently return raw strings or lists without any metadata about token consumption that could be aggregated.
adrs.decision: Define a TokenUsage namedtuple or dataclass (TokenUsage(total_tokens: int, prompt_tokens: int, completion_tokens: int)) and use it consistently across all provider implementations and aggregation points to ensure uniform handling of usage metrics throughout the system.
adrs.rationale: This approach provides a consistent interface for token tracking that can be easily extended in future if more granular breakdowns are needed. It follows Python best practices with named tuples for simple data containers that maintain immutability while being easy to work with.
consequences: All LLM providers must return TokenUsage objects alongside their primary responses
Engine methods can reliably extract and aggregate token information using standardized fields
API layer has consistent format for reporting usage metrics to consumers
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Use plain dictionaries instead of structured types - rejected because it's less type-safe, harder to maintain, and error-prone when accessing nested keys
Alternative 2 - Create custom TokenUsage class with methods for aggregation - rejected because it introduces unnecessary complexity for simple data tracking use case
Alternative 3 - Return raw token counts without structure (int) - rejected because it loses detailed breakdown information that's valuable for cost analysis and optimization

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol for benchmarking hooks to track method calls
responsibilities: Provide interface for tracking method call start and end events
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol for caching hooks to manage cached results
responsibilities: Provide interface for retrieving and storing cached results
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Proxy class for instrumenting object methods with timing and metrics tracking
responsibilities: Wrap target objects to intercept method calls and add instrumentation
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Main engine for answering queries using LLMs and other components
responsibilities: Orchestrate retrieval and generation components to answer queries
Handle cloud caching of results
Manage ingestion pipeline
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide high-level interface to interact with the KRAG engine
Handle configuration and collection management
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.purpose: Concrete implementation of VisionProvider for Ollama services
responsibilities: Describe images using Ollama vision models
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, base_url: str | None) -> None
describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol for benchmarking hooks to track method calls
responsibilities: Provide interface for tracking method call start and end events
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: CachingHook
components.purpose: Protocol for caching hooks to manage cached results
responsibilities: Provide interface for retrieving and storing cached results
interfaces: get_cached_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
cache_result(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict, result: Any) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Proxy class for instrumenting object methods with timing and metrics tracking
responsibilities: Wrap target objects to intercept method calls and add instrumentation
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Main engine for answering queries using LLMs and other components
responsibilities: Orchestrate retrieval and generation components to answer queries
Handle cloud caching of results
Manage ingestion pipeline
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None
point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any
_fast_index_code_files(manifest: Any, source_dir: Path, progress: Callable[[str], None] | None) -> None

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide high-level interface to interact with the KRAG engine
Handle configuration and collection management
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

integration_points: OpenAIChat — chat and chat_stream methods need to return token usage metadata
OllamaChat — chat and chat_stream methods need to parse and return token usage from API responses
AnthropicChat — chat and chat_stream methods need to return token usage metadata
CohereChat — chat and chat_stream methods need to return token usage metadata
OpenAIEmbedding — embed and embed_batch methods need to return token usage metadata
OllamaEmbedding — embed and embed_batch methods need to parse and return token usage from API responses
CohereEmbedding — embed and embed_batch methods need to return token usage metadata
CohereRerank — rerank method needs to return token usage metadata
OllamaRerank — rerank method needs to parse and return token usage from API responses
OpenAIVision — describe_image method needs to return token usage metadata
AnthropicVision — describe_image method needs to return token usage metadata
OllamaVision — describe_image method needs to parse and return token usage from API responses

artifacts.purpose: Defines base protocols for LLM providers including chat, embedding, reranking, and vision interfaces
Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance with hook-based method interception
Answer class representing a structured response with optional token count tracking

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source

phases.name: Implement TokenUsageHook
phases.objective: Add a benchmark hook to capture LLM token usage from API responses
deliverables: TokenUsageHook class implementing BenchmarkHook protocol in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
key_risks: API response format inconsistencies across LLM providers

phases.name: Instrument LLM Providers
phases.objective: Wrap chat and embedding methods in LLM provider classes to track token usage
key_risks: Performance regression from additional wrapping logic
Inconsistent token usage data formats across providers

phases.name: Integrate Token Tracking in Engine
phases.objective: Capture and expose token usage during query answering process
deliverables: TokenUsage retrieval logic in FitzKragEngine.answer method
API endpoint additions to return token usage data
key_risks: Breaking changes if not carefully integrated with existing answer flow
Potential memory bloat from storing large numbers of TokenUsage objects

risks.description: Token usage data may not be consistently available across all LLM providers, especially non-OpenAI models that don't return usage fields in responses
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific parsing logic for each supported LLM client to extract token usage from API responses
risks.contingency: Fall back to estimating tokens using text length when direct usage data is unavailable, or skip tracking if no usage information is present in the response
risks.verification: Assert that token usage hook processes responses from all supported LLM providers and gracefully handles missing usage fields

risks.description: Instrumentation of LLM provider methods might introduce performance overhead or break existing behavior if not carefully implemented
risks.mitigation: Use the existing InstrumentedProxy system for wrapping rather than manual instrumentation, and test thoroughly in staging environments before production deployment
risks.contingency: Implement feature flag to disable token tracking during performance-sensitive operations or if issues arise post-deployment
risks.verification: Assert that existing LLM provider behaviors are unchanged and no performance degradation exceeds 5% under normal load

risks.description: Token usage data could potentially expose internal system details or usage patterns to unauthorized users if not properly secured in logs or APIs
risks.mitigation: Ensure token usage tracking only occurs within secure, internal observability flows and that sensitive usage information is filtered from public outputs
risks.contingency: Implement access controls for any token usage endpoints or logging mechanisms to restrict visibility to authorized personnel only
risks.verification: Assert that all token usage data is logged with appropriate security context and not exposed through public APIs

risks.description: Inconsistencies in how different LLM providers report token usage (e.g., some return total_tokens, others don't) can lead to inaccurate aggregation or analysis
risks.mitigation: Standardize token data extraction using a unified protocol that normalizes differences across provider responses before storing in TokenUsage objects
risks.contingency: Implement fallback logic and logging for cases where usage fields are missing or malformed, allowing continued operation with partial tracking
risks.verification: Assert that token data is consistently extracted across all provider types and gracefully handles missing fields without breaking the system

recommended_contingencies: Implement feature flag to disable token tracking during performance-sensitive operations or if issues arise post-deployment
Add access controls for any token usage endpoints or logging mechanisms to restrict visibility to authorized personnel only
Implement fallback logic and logging for cases where usage fields are missing or malformed, allowing continued operation with partial tracking
